reviews.review_description:
This is one of my favorite VR games to play. It’s so fun and I love all of the different mechanics. The fighting style is amazing and the dungeon runs are so fun. There are some things that I recommend that the developers add and change.

First, is the amount of XP it takes to get to level up in between levels 50-60. It takes so long to level up and is really annoying to try to get a mythic weapon when I can barely level up. 

Second, I recommend that you get more XP from completing level 7 runs. It doesn’t make it worth it to go through a level 7 that takes like 20 mins and only get 10,000 XP.

Third, I love to play this game with my friends, but I find that solo player is a bit boring. I would recommend adding in some type of story line or something that can make playing solo player more fun and interesting. 

Fourth, I would love it if the developers added in pets. I love the dog by the merchants, so it would be great if you could find pets on the dungeons that have unique abilities but, you have to try and tame them. If the developers do add pets, they should also add pet clothing, so you can customize how your pet looks.

Overall, this is an amazing and super fun game that I would totally recommend buying.

I’ve been playing Dungeons of Eternity through Horizon+ and have had a blast! The gameplay is fun, the combat feels real, and it’s even better when playing with friends. Every session ends up being a memorable adventure 😁

Shout-out to Discord mod G.E.N.E. I recently teamed up with him for a dungeon run, and his passion, excitement, and genuine love for the game were contagious. His enthusiasm was actually the deciding factor that convinced me to buy the game yesterday. 😎

I just found a Room coin!! 🥳  I hope it brings us all good luck 😼

If you’re thinking about getting the game, I’d definitely recommend it. See you in the dungeons! ⚔️🛡️😈

reviews.review_title: THE best game on Meta Quest!!! A+++
reviews.review_description: WOW… this game has it all! Mystery, Action, Adventure, Horror, Magic, Dragons, Monsters, Fighting and more! BUT the best part is the Up to 4 player option… you get to meet amazing people from all over the world!!! Just choose Join a Dungeon in progress or host your own room with friends or opt for anyone to join and I guarantee you that not only will you will have a blast but you will learn with and work with the most kind and generous players of any game ive ever experienced… and all in stunning VR!!! Speaking of which the set design, graphics, music and characters are just another level… you can tell the DEVs love what they do and took so much pride in this! This game is a MUST HAVE! (see ya in a dungeon! But watch out for the flying screaming flaming skull thingys… those things are terrifying!!!) Oh AND if you find a single gold coin on the ground, make sure to exclaim ROOM COIN when picking it up as I hear that brings your team good luck!

Graphics: 8.5/10

Gameplay:10/10

Combat:9.8/10

Tracking:10/10

Physics:7.9/10

Immersion:10/10

Updates?:once in a while

Addictive?: Yes

OVERALL: Very good game that I recommend,I started playing it once and then I just kept playing it everyday. 10/10 game

This is one of the games that has immense potential to grow even more. At its current stage, it's already so replayable, with multiplayer, it turns into an even better game to socialize with others. I've met a few friends through this game and I've been playing with them semi-regularly. 

Of course, everyone has tons of ideas on what to add to the game, but one thing I'd honestly like to have are some sort of gauntlets. I play VR for the sake of both entertainment and exercise, and I feel like having gauntlets that may be extended a bit to better hit enemies with will add SO MUCH more cool movements. 

Another suggestion would be to add incentive for players to play melee instead of most people just throwing weapons and using bows and crossbows. Because the enemies are a bit riskier to take on in melee.

Lastly, I think there should be a 'no kids mode', for obvious reasons. About 80% of the kids are annoying, loud, childish (surprising, I know), and can just kick you for absolutely no reason in the middle of a dungeon.

But overall, I look forward to my third mythic! I hope I don't botch it like my first two.

100+ hours played.

I initially picked this up through Horizon+ and honestly didn't expect much. Man, was I wrong.

Dungeons of Eternity has become the game I almost exclusively play on Quest now. The 4-player co-op is fantastic, but what keeps me coming back is the amount of detail the developers have put into the game. The physics, satisfying combat, beautiful environments, randomized dungeons, and huge variety of room layouts make every run feel familiar yet different.

I've made a lot of friends through this game. Some of my favourite moments have been completely unexpected: random karaoke dungeon crawls, veteran players teaching new tricks, hilarious bugs, and legendary players somehow carrying the entire team when everyone else is down on a no-revive run.

The combat is incredibly satisfying. Headshots matter, the weapon variety is great, and the skeletons are probably my favourite enemies in any VR game. Whether you're pinning them to a spear, shooting their skulls off with a bow, or completely shattering them, it never gets old. Throwing weapons feel amazing, and the optional assist system makes you feel like an absolute legend! Magic is also surprisingly deep, with a great selection of staffs. My personal favourites are Telekinesis and Ice, but Fireball, Force, Chrono, Shockwave, and Healing all have their place.

Compared to other VR games, I'd put this above Blade & Sorcery simply because it feels like a complete game and works brilliantly in co-op. It's one of the few games that convinced me to buy extra BOBOVR battery packs just so I could play longer.

The community is generally awesome. You'll occasionally run into screecher kids, but they're usually kicked quickly and it's easy enough to find another lobby.

A few criticisms:

- Levelling slows down quite a bit around levels 40–60.
- I'd love more things to spend gold on.
- More weapons, enemies, cosmetics, and weapon customization would be awesome.
- Trading or gifting gear would be a cool addition.
- I've experienced a few glitches, but nothing that a reboot or a little patience couldn't fix.

As a fantasy nerd, I'd also love to see more fantasy-inspired cosmetics in the future. Witcher, Lord of the Rings, Berserk-inspired styles, that sort of thing.

And for any new players reading this: if you find a room coin, yell "ROOM COIN!" immediately. It's basically the law.

If someone bought a Quest tomorrow and asked me what game to get first, Dungeons of Eternity would be near the top of my list. The story is there to support the game modes, but the gameplay, co-op experience, and replayability are what make this game special.

developer_response.body: Greatest.Review.Ever. :)  Thank you for the feedback too!  We have a lot planned for DoE including a lot of stuff on your list.

reviews.review_description: I love this game somtimes there is this houng boy who is bong water 22 he kept cussing many times at this young girl and kept saying her name. Was Brian when clearly it was not people just ruin the game and I do feel bad Telford that girl and I hope he dms her on the app to say sorry if I could I whould give zero stars

This game has become my favourite game to play because it never gets repetitive, there is always different things! This has replaced sail vr as my favourite game because sail is just repetitive and there is only one map, but in this game there is a lot of different weapons you can get with some interesting potions! Five stars and I definitely recommend it if you like fun stuff that you can still grind in!

If you’re on the fence, get it. You won’t regret it! The graphics are super cool (designs of dungeons, chests, keys—the lighting, etc) and the weapons and cosmetics are cool. Don’t consider myself a gamer and this is my first dungeon crawl game ever but now I play it every day.  It’s a fun game to play with others, although you can play alone. I did that to practice the bow.

reviews.review_title: My favorite game of all times!!!
reviews.review_description: Me and my family (from NY to FL and around the world)  meet in the dungeons every night no matter where we are and enjoy destroying the zombies, wizards, bosses while collecting money and treasures as we talk about how our day went. Laughter, screaming and yelling “I got your back” through the night has become a ritual. We cannot be at the same place at the same time always physically, but here we can game wise. . We even relax at the end of our game by the fireplace making believe we’re drinking knead. It’s so much fun!!!! I’m so happy this game was created. Helps everyone to continue the family/friend communication alive. 🥰💕

My family and I have been playing every night after work for two years.  It’s so fun watching how we all work as a team. If you are looking for an exhilarating, adventurous and beautiful game…THIS IS IT! I look forward to playing all the time. This is how my family and I have been choosing to wind down after work, we even sit at the fire place and discuss our adventure after! Super amazing wholesome fun. DUNGEONS OF ETERNITY 4 EVER !!
ദ്ദി( • ᴗ < )

Mythic weapons ruined this game online , instead of a TEAM of heroes with melee weapons you now have a group of individuals stealing your kills with magic projectiles from across the room . You have to have the exact same mythic weapons as everyone else to even get kills . No fun unique builds just a mythic bow or spear to steal kills from afar and mythic dagger to use the “ghost strike” exploit or whatever they call it where you use specific settings to spam magic projectiles from the dagger. No more awesome melee battles to get my blood going just mythic projectile spamming. This was literally the only game I played despite owning like a hundred now it’s no fun online 
RIP DoE.

Wow. So good. Feels like Diablo in VR. Some of the enemies in higher difficulty are terrifying and it feels good to turn them to broken bones and dust. You can tell these devs Actually play games.. And then multiplayer : O incredible

Reaaally fun especially with friends. Not glitchy, even with big levels. 

However, I just finished a piramid level by myself while in a "friendroom" before my friends could join, and the level did not end. It just kept playing the triumph end song. I had to manually leave, and I did not get any of the XP/coins/etc I had collected. Thats really a bummer! 

Also, I have experienced a few times that there was a chest behind bars, and no lever anywhere to lift the bars. 

Sometimes also there seem to not be enough keys for the amount of chests, is that just a random thing that can happen or did we miss something then?

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review and feedback.  At some point we made the "Always Run" option in the control settings toggled ON by default.  This saves you from wearing out/breaking your thumbstick just to run.  If you want to have control over running, then go into the game settings > controls and disable auto run.

reviews.review_title: Great Game — Please Improve Friend Syste
reviews.review_description:
Hey! I bought this game and honestly it’s beautiful. I really enjoy playing it, especially the multiplayer — it makes the experience amazing. If the game had a little more brutality and stronger combat effects, it would be perfect for me, but even like this it’s still very good.

I did encounter one big problem though: the friend request system. It’s very hard to access and send friend requests. It would be much better if players could easily send friend requests directly while we are in the lobby together. New players don’t understand how to add friends, and because of this I lost many good people I met in the game since none of us knew how to send or accept friend requests.

At first I didn’t want to buy the game because I wasn’t convinced. When I started playing, I also didn’t like it much because I didn’t understand anything. But then I met some really nice players who explained how everything works, and once I understood the mechanics, I started loving the game.

My advice for new players: if you want to try this game, play at least until level 10 before deciding to quit or refund it. Or try playing a couple of co-op multiplayer matches — that’s where the game really shines.

Overall, I really like the game a lot. The only thing I would improve is the friend request system and maybe add a bit more brutality and stronger effects to make the combat feel more realistic. But still, great game!

reviews.review_title: One of the best RPGs on Meta but ..
reviews.review_description:
No offline mode.

So when meta shuts the servers down like with Larcenauts you cannot play this game anymore . 

It’s gonna be worthless unless they make an offline mode. 

I don’t even play this coop.
Besides that best atmosphere and sword play on quest

developer_response.body: We have offline ready for the next update.  I hope you'll adjust your review accordingly when it's released :)  Feel free to reach out to us at www.othergate.com/support

reviews.review_description:
This is not just a game, but a whole new world. Enter an experience where you can be who you've always wanted to be, weild the weapons you've always dreamed of and team up with players who become lifelong friends. 

Face untold dangers, triumph over creatures of nightmare then sit around the fireplace sharing stories with your fellow adventurers.

If you want to play a video game, there's lots out there. If you want to live a never ending adventure play Dungeons of Eternity.

I’m not trying to glaze the game just because of the good graphics, but this game is seriously a great game. Normally I like historically accurate games but this one is still great. Even though it’s normally $30 it goes on sale a lot so it’s mostly $20 which is kind of expensive for a vr game but it’s worth every penny.

Confession: when this launched in 2023 I hated it. Called it Dungeons of Mediocrity. Two years on, it has somehow become one of my top six VR games of all time. 

The core loop hasn't changed dramatically but everything around it has tightened. Combat is snappier, hit detection is solid, swings feel real. Enemy and room variety have expanded. 

Four-player co-op (up from three) makes a bigger difference than I expected, and the haptic-vest integration is best in class. Feeling coins land in my pouch and arrows draw from my shoulder turned out to be hard to play without. 

The community is unusually decent; random quick-join lobbies are reliably fun. Magic still arrives too late and feels tacked on, but those are tweaks, not deal-breakers. Twenty hours across Steam and Quest in three weeks, and I'm still telling myself "one more run." This is the best version of itself.

For the full review, search YouTube for 6DOF Reviews Dungeons of Eternity!

This was a fun game. The first time. It's interactive, the levels are well-designed. But there are no save points on a level. So you get to the final boss challenge. Get killed. Then do the WHOLE THING again. And again. And again. So ultimately very, very boring. If you got to respawn where you died and adapted to your skill level there would be much more play value.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review and feedback.  We hate to see people struggling with the game.    There are endless random dungeons to play, so there are no save points in any of them.  That said you can always save a dungeon you've failed if you want to go back and try it again... but the dungeons change every 20 minutes.

In solo play you get the "last chance" mode.. where if you die, you have like 20 seconds to kill another enemy, or drink a health potion.  Additionally, falling into a pit or some of the enemy deaths simply respawn you - the point of the game is to have fun, not frustrate you.  Make sure you have a full inventory of potions.  There are usually too many health potions sitting in the dungeon at the end of a run, especially on low difficulties.

Until you get the hang of things, make sure you're only playing on Difficulty Tier 1 and pick shorter dungeons from the dungeon scanner.  Keep in mind, even if you do lose a dungeon, you're keep the weapon blueprints you found in chests and can come back and fabricate them - which increases your damage and success for the next dungeon.

Additionally, there are tons of YouTube videos with tips and tricks (SilverTonguedDevil) that can give you hints for playing - like use a ranged weapon when you're new to keep the enemies at a distance.  Finally, our Discord server is full of great folks that would be willing to help a new player get started.  You can also contact us directly.  Good luck.

Some of the best physics I have seen.
Great combat.

Goooooooooooosddddddddddddgameeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeereeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeerreeeeeeeeeerreeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeereeeeeeeeeeeeeeereeeeeeeedddddddceeeeeeeeeeeee were i was a kid and i was a kid in the shower and i was a
i've been playing for a little bit over 2 months and even though sometimes the kids can be frustrating the older ones are pretty coordinated and make a great team

reviews.review_title: Very close to being a must buy!
reviews.review_description:
I really hope the devs read this, I have a lot to say and I think they could stand to hear it.

First of all, the tutorialization of this game is abysmal. It has a relatively barebones tutorial for the intro but then the other mission types, the crystal hunt for example, only tell you mechanically what you’re trying to do, not what will happen. I deposited all the crystals which prompted a “defend the pyramid!” Popup, and summoned a giant red skeleton wizard that could very much hurt me but I couldn’t hurt at all. I’m genuinely not sure what I was supposed to do beyond kill his minions, but I failed the mission almost immediately with no warning of how much health the objective had left, only intermittent progress updates. The whole game is like this, it could stand to have much more complete tutorials.

Next is the archery and general ranged combat. In general, vr ranged combat needs a whole lot of aim assist, and this game is in desperate need. The archery  is too demanding of perfect aim in a format that is inherently difficult to aim a bow in. The staves are really cool! But I whiff maybe 30% of my shots because the hitboxes for every weapon are tiny! Which funnels into my next point.

The combat in general is very well conceived, but implemented to middling success. There’s too little feedback and it’s really easy to get caught on enemies completely negating a swing. Games like asgards wrath and until you fall have very similar systems, but the most perfected has been walking dead saints and sinners, they’re responsive and engaging and it feels good to get close to something. The enemies in this game feel bad to hit, and the weapons, at least early on, don’t feel good to swing.

My last critique is the whole scifi framing device and base aesthetic. It’s not really in the trailers or description, and caught me off guard which is fine but it feels very poorly connected. My health indicator is glowing magic gloves, I’m using fantasy weapons, I heal with potions and spend gold, but the map is a scifi hologram and I use the gold on a glorified 3D printer. I think you either need to fully lean into it and have these fantasy weapons be scifi looking, or do away with it completely. A fantasy hologram map, a magic crafting table instead of a fabricator, a war table instead of a holomap etc. it seems very much like something that should’ve been weeded out in the concepting phase.

I understand that it’s very difficult to make an online game like this and also nail all those other elements, but it’s critical with BR games to have them be responsive and engaging, which this game gets SO close to being. For now I might refund it and see what else I can play with my friends.
The physics in this game are unmatched, the game does have a hefty price tag but it may be the greatest dungeon raider in vr to date
Got to appreciate the adventure/dungeon style play. I have always enjoyed this type of game. But VR has made it quite pleasant to act out.

I was wondering if we can get new hair styles and clothes for the female avatar in the game I would like to make her look like a Viking maybe. Also please tell the guy that created Nova the wolf in the game Is a *ucking genius because that wolf is so cute and fluffy 🥺. Oh one more thing keep up the good work this game is dope. Oh and I would love a mythic Ice Staff please that would be totally dope.
Love this game. It's a lot of fun being immersed in this amazing looking world!

I’ve been playing VR co-op games on my Meta Quest 3 for some years now. I’ve done zombies, alien bugs & robots and my wife and I were up for a new game. After doing quite some research I came up with Dungeons of Eternity and boy were we blown away! It’s very clear from the graphics and gameplay that this is the state of the art VR experience as it should be. The game offers all a Dungeons and Dragons fan could want and we love the game. It offers a very interesting level up system where after reaching level 60 you can choose a Mystic weapon (best weapons in the game) and then start over from level 1 retaining all your weapons, stats and perks you already gained. This is brilliant and the map are generated in different ways so you can always find a new nice map to play. Highly recommended!

Dungeons of Eternity is one of the best VR co-op games I’ve played. The combat feels amazing, the weapons are fun to use, and every dungeon run feels different. Playing with friends is an absolute blast, and the progression/reward system keeps me coming back. The devs clearly put a lot of care into the game and continue improving it. Highly recommend for anyone who loves fantasy combat and VR adventures!

Let me start by saying this game is great and I enjoy tf outta playing it. But what's the point of the tier system if I get the same XP for running a tier 5 as I get for a tier 7? There needs to be some point for doing the higher tier dungeons. Once you reach the recommended tier 7 there's no bonus for playing that tier like there is earlier in the game. If you can add bonus multipliers once we reach the tier 7 recommended level that would be awesome. The level system is great up to level 45 then it's just a neverending grind to get the last 15 levels if you're going to make the XP 100,000 to make level 60 can you please add higher level dungeons!

reviews.review_title: The best game to play with friends
reviews.review_description: Im not glazing definitely not 😫😩 I love doing it in lobby’s too

reviews.review_title: A single room coin brought me luck.
reviews.review_description: I love this game. I haven't really played anything else since January and it's May. The people are more in the friendly ratio and mostly cooperative. The gameplay is thoughtful and class creation is very flexible. The only thing that could possibly make it better would be to give the mimics legs and make the reaper faster. Also if taller people could equip a saddle for shorter players to ride on them. Keep up the great work!

reviews.review_title: I Love Game! So so so much!
reviews.review_description:
Dungeons of Eternity has easily become one of, if not, my favourite games in VR! It seriously nails that dungeon crawling feeling with many weapons and skill boosts (after a ton of crystal hunting) that you can unlock! It’s also fun to play in long session and a good way to meet good people, if you’re willing to find them.

One of my favourite parts is finding the little goblin and looting him, I love how everyone immediately throws everything they have at it. No way your getting away from me little goblin! (Yes I’m calling it out, stop me.)

The Dungeon design is another big highlight for me. The rooms, especially the boss ones, stand out a lot! They immediately feel more intense and memorable than the standard rooms. You can tell they put so much effort into making each room look different and rewarding to find!

But what really surprised me is the community! I’ve met a lot of genuinely great people whiling playing that have helped me too. And yes it has kids, like any other game, but I find most of them a good. Whether it’s teaming up with random players or just having fun with friends during a run, the social side of the game adds a whole new layer that many similar games don’t get too doing.

Overall, dungeons of eternity is a must-play if you enjoy combat in VR, co-op experiences, and dungeon exploration. It’s fun, social, and always keeping you coming back for just one more run.

Overall rating: 11/10
The best vr combat I have ever witnessed, the most amazing community, outstanding dev team, and incredible vibes! 100/10
Great over all but the friends system and inviting friends is a pain to deal with

This game is great! I play it with two of my friends so you can share a lot of laughs as well as all the different mechanics being great too. The only falt I see is that this is not something I would play on my own unless I was a hardcore player as it would get too repetitive. However this game is great and even better with friends.

Since installing the game ive played every day and its been great. Fast to jump into a lobby and get right into the fun. Simple controls for anyone to pick up but the item physics is what hooked me. Wanna parry your own weapon into an enemy? you can. Wanna play baseball with a bomb? you can. Definitely worth its price

Took an hour to download the game but only tried for 15 min and it gave me heavy motion sickness, trying to apply for a refund and only received an auto response saying error to reimburse. No way to contact live support to fix the issue and no way to report the refund error!!!

developer_response.body:
Refunds are handled by Meta, not developers in the Meta ecosystem.  Developers have nothing to do with store operations, downloads, purchases, and/or refunds.  There's not a developer in VR that has live support.  We offer support through our website www.othergate.com/support or on our discord server.  We're happy to help you there - and I think you'll find our support is very prompt if not close to "live".

It's my guess you skipped the tutorial and the comfort options.  Turn off smooth movement and switch to Teleport or Blink mode in the game settings.  Plenty of people that have motion sickness playing VR are able to enjoy our game successfully.  Please reach out if you need any more help.  Sorry I can't help you with the refund.

reviews.review_description: This game has an almost infinite amount of dungeons, and I just love looking in finding room keys!

Immersive, well-made, first person dungeon crawler. Thoroughly enjoy this gamvery fun
e!

Play this game with my kids. We team up and have a blast! They whip my butt, but it’s fun. Maybe an old man upgrade DOE? I need an edge and I have the money they don’t lol!! Great game. Thank you for the fun!

reviews.review_title: Do I even need to explain myself?
reviews.review_description: Dungeons of eternity is one of the BEST dungeon games I have ever played. The enemies, the spells, EVERYTHING! This is the best $15 dollar purchases ever. Will ALWAYS play again

developer_response.body: Thanks for the nice review.  How do we earn the extra two stars? :)

reviews.review_description:
It could be a perfect game if not a skeleton king boss. Even experienced players on youtube prefer to spam it with range weapons instead of going melee.
Its attacks often goes literally through weapons and sometimes even through walls. Dude once one shotted me being paralyzed by poison status I still have no idea how (maybe secret mental attack I dont know) and I literally saw paralyzed skeleton near to my dead body and no other enemies in the room…
Why there is no option before creating a game just to remove some enemies from spawn list?

Also, there is no normal 2 handed swords. Tye only weapon which feels like 2 handed sword is - a spear. But spear often gets stuck in enemies leading to fast travel back to the lobby.
Sometimes spear just can fall out of your hands and stuck somewhere between enemies

Another big problem is - throwing weapons assist. Many games lost because when its critical to throw something at a mage right in front of me, the weapon goes to a random spider

Interface is another pain. To grab a two handed weapon with left hand its better to raise the weapon over head before grabbing it - in other cases you will open your inventory, grab your one handed weapon, grab a potion from the floor, literally anything but not weapon’s grab

This game has brought my childhood dream to life! I haven't wanted to stop playing, the community are amazing people that seem to all love this Sci-fi D&D adventure that has exceeded any game out there. Please continue to update and nurture this beautiful masterpiece into the future of Dungeon gaming!

GoldenWrenchy
I'm enjoying the playthroughs of a lot of dungeons and weapons. Want to see more. I want to there to be a better dungeons then what we have already.

This is an awesome game, but I feel that it doesn't get enough updates, the latest update for January just added some random dice, and the December update added some bomb no one was using. Please  update the game to add things like new dungeons, weapons, and maybe even some armor that you collect from chests, please.

developer_response.body: Hey thanks for the review and feedback.  Rest assured we're working on updates.  We're a small team and some of these updates we've been doing are really big - like the one we're currently working on.  There was a developer video recently talking about it.  Additionally, you can always see what we've shipped previously from our update website here:  https://www.othergate.com/updates

reviews.review_description: Dungeons of eternity is by far my favorite game but we need an easier way to get to level 60. It is just such a grind to get all that xp. Maybe you could please add some quests that give us xp boosts gold or weapons. You could also add more bosses like a dragon or hydra. I have seen hundreds of people ask for all that stuff. Still an awesome game 100 of 5 stars all day.

reviews.review_title: Great Game Bustling Community Must Buy
reviews.review_description:
I came for the dungeons. I stayed for everything else.
The combat is genuinely physical in a way that rewards investment. I watched a Dungeons of Eternity YouTube video teaching Escrima dual wielding martial arts techniques that actually translate into the game’s mechanics. That’s not something you say about most VR titles. The physics are that good. Every weapon type feels different in your hand, and the moment you find your build the combination of weapons and perks that matches how your brain works, the dungeons open up in a completely new way.

I need to specifically mention throwing daggers. The way they feel leaving your hand, the arc, the impact when they land, it is so satisfying that I have genuinely started looking up knife throwing as a real life hobby.

What I keep coming back to though is the community. I’ve been in a lot of gaming communities. It’s nice to see a community of this size with this much creative investment in the world they’re playing in. Players have built guilds with full origin stories, crypt libraries of lore, YouTube channels, PvP tournaments, monthly events with prize pools. All of it community driven with zero in-game guild mechanics forcing it. That kind of organic culture doesn’t happen by accident. It happens when a game gives people something worth caring about.

Othergate built something worth caring about. Six people. Independent studio. Full creative freedom. You can feel that in every design decision. The game has a point of view and it commits to it.

I’m three weeks in, level 50, playing every single day since I started, and already thinking about the content I want to make, the guild I want to build, and the history I want to document. That’s not what I expected when I teleported into my first dungeon.

Whatever you came here looking for, I hope you find it. And I hope Eternity surprises you the way it surprised me.

Long Live Legana.

— gamersrealm_og

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review.. probably one of the best reviews we've ever gotten - especially since it focuses on the community.  We love our community and we're working hard on the next update (even a bit harder after reading this one! )

reviews.review_description: I got a room coin and it brought me good luck to the dungeon I love this game so much i will never stop playing it

The game is amazing don’t get me wrong, however there should be more of incentive once reaching lvl 50 because it takes way to long to lvl up to 60. Maybe add in harder dungeons once you reach lvl 50 to where you get higher base XP to keep the lvl up just as fast. Going from lvl 50 to 60 takes such a long time because all the dungeons base XP is no more than 3700. Please see what can be done about this as the game is just grind grind grind but no incentives once reaching a certain lvl.
i really like the graphics and the physics in the game you also do not have to pay money to be good you just have to grind.

Dungeons of Eternity is one of those VR games that just feels right. The first time I played it, I didn’t think about learning a system or grinding mechanics. I felt like I was genuinely going on an adventure. Each dungeon run is unpredictable, and that "what’s around the corner?" feeling stays with you, no matter how often you play.

What really stands out for me are the weapons, especially the staffs. Using magic in VR feels natural, and the staffs in this game are a blast. Blasting enemies, controlling space, and feeling powerful without going overboard is something the game gets right. It encourages you to try new things instead of sticking to one boring playstyle.

The crossbows are another highlight. There’s something really satisfying about landing a clean shot amid the chaos. Reloading while enemies press you adds enough stress to make every hit feel earned. It’s one of those weapons that makes you feel skilled when you succeed, not just lucky.

Honestly, my favorite weapon is the knockback shield. It turns panic into comedy. When you’re swarmed and send enemies flying with a shield bash, it never stops being funny or satisfying. It’s defensive, aggressive, and chaotic all at once, making for some of the most memorable moments in the game.

The gameplay loop is simple but well-executed. You go in, fight, adapt, survive, and come out feeling accomplished. The dungeons offer a challenge, but they don’t feel unfair. When you fail, it’s usually because of a mistake on your part, which just makes you want to dive back in and do better next time.

One of the best parts of Dungeons of Eternity is the people you meet. I ended up playing with friends like Gene, Mistaken, Brian, and many others. Running dungeons together, saving each other in critical moments, and laughing when things go wrong makes the game more than just a VR experience; it turns into a shared memory.

Those friendships really change how the game feels. A good run isn’t just about loot or levels; it’s about teamwork and those moments when everything somehow works out. Even the bad runs end up being fun because you’re experiencing them together.

At the end of the day, Dungeons of Eternity isn’t just great because of the staffs, crossbows, or the knockback shield, even though those are fantastic. It’s great because of the stories you create, the chaos you survive, and the friends you make along the way. It’s the kind of VR game you keep returning to, not out of obligation, but because you genuinely want to.

Shield strikes, bodies soar
Crossbows snap, magic ignites
Friends fight side by side

It’s an amazing diversive game also if u hate spiders turn on arachnophobia mode and your all set best game ten out of ten
!

reviews.review_title: Amazing game, but voice chat issue
reviews.review_description:
This game is absolutely amazing. Honestly, it’s one of the best VR games I have ever played. Every time I come home from work, I’m looking forward to playing it. It’s exactly the kind of game that makes owning a VR headset worth it. I would honestly buy a Meta Quest 3 just for this game alone, as I don’t really play anything else.
Unfortunately, I have a problem with voice chat. At the start of the game I can hear other players normally, but after a few minutes their voices start lagging and then I can’t hear them at all. Other players seem to communicate normally with each other, but I hear nothing.
I already tried resetting the headset, reinstalling the game, and I have a stable Wi-Fi connection, so the issue doesn’t seem to be on my side.
Does anyone else have the same problem? Is there any fix?
i love it, amazing game!
The game is a fun dungeon crawler. If you enjoy those types of games then I think you'll enjoy this.
Very fun would recommend to anyone.
I have been playing it with my friends and it is so much fun

It's one of the best fighting multiplayer games on VR. Really good graphics and challenging content. Great upgrade system and design for characters. Awesome monsters. But I find the game tends to give me the creeps real bad, and I normally love playing the Walking Dead on VR, which says something. Because the game has no sunlight and you are always in darkness, I find it plays negatively on my mood after a while.
Actually love this game bro the level system is so good and unlocking weapons is fun. The mechanics can be satisfying and fun and this game is just an overall blast. 100% recommend
Please add katanas
this is my second favorite game it is soooooooo fun
Loved playing it with my friends
I love the physics and the combat is so satisfying.
I've tried quite a few games, but none has captivated me as much as Dungeon of Eternity so far. Great game, great community.
